summary: IDF forces killed a Golani officer and eliminated Hezbollah operatives in Deir Siryan, southern Lebanon, while also destroying a 200‑meter underground tunnel in Majdal Zoun. Hezbollah fired at Israeli troops on the Syrian Golan front, causing no Israeli casualties. Diplomatic activity includes a reported US‑Iran agreement to halt attacks and Iran’s commemoration of the Sardasht chemical massacre.
key_points: IDF killed Cpt. David Hazutt and wounded another soldier during a clash with a Hezbollah gunman in Deir Siryan, southern Lebanon, then struck Hezbollah sites in the area (Source: @manniefabian/58695).
The Israeli Air Force hit Hezbollah operatives armed with RPGs in the Nabatieh area and destroyed a Hezbollah rocket launcher threatening Israeli forces (Source: @manniefabian/58691).
IDF demolished a large Hezbollah underground tunnel in Majdal Zoun, measuring over 200 meters in length and more than 25 meters deep (Source: @manniefabian/58701).
A militant fired at Israeli forces in the southern Golan region of Syria; the shooter escaped and no Israeli casualties were reported (Source: @New_security8200/202140).
Israel reported the killing of three senior Hamas commanders, including a naval police chief, in a strike in Gaza (Source: @kianmeli1/88219).
The Palestinian army detonated residential buildings near a mosque in Bani Suhail, east of Khan Younis in Gaza (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373489).
The United States and Iran reportedly agreed to halt kinetic operations and plan a meeting in Qatar to discuss Strait of Hormuz issues (Source: @VahidOnline/76734).

sections.executive_summary: IDF forces killed a Golani officer and eliminated Hezbollah operatives in Deir Siryan, southern Lebanon, while also destroying a 200‑meter underground tunnel in Majdal Zoun. Hezbollah engaged Israeli troops on the Syrian Golan front without causing casualties, and Israel struck senior Hamas leaders in Gaza. Diplomatic moves include a reported US‑Iran agreement to cease attacks and Iran’s commemoration of the Sardasht chemical massacre.

points: Iran marked the 39th anniversary of the Sardasht chemical attack, calling for accountability of Western actors who supported Saddam Hussein's use of chemical weapons (Source: @presstv/196058).
Iranian officials reported a US‑Iran agreement to stop kinetic operations and planned a technical meeting in Qatar to address Strait of Hormuz tensions (Source: @VahidOnline/76734).
A photo exhibition opened in Tehran to commemorate the Sardasht attack anniversary (Source: @presstv/196060).
Iranian media highlighted US‑Iran coordination to allow free movement of naval vessels pending technical talks (Source: @VahidOnline/76734).

Cpt. David Hazutt, 21, of the Golani Brigade's 12th Battalion, was killed in a clash with a Hezbollah gunman in Deir Siryan, southern Lebanon; another soldier was lightly injured and later treated (Source: @manniefabian/58695).
The Israeli Air Force struck Hezbollah operatives armed with RPGs in the Nabatieh area and destroyed a Hezbollah rocket launcher that posed a threat to Israeli forces (Source: @manniefabian/58691).
IDF engineers demolished a Hezbollah underground tunnel in Majdal Zoun, measuring over 200 meters long and more than 25 meters deep, to remove a strategic threat (Source: @manniefabian/58701).
A militant fired at Israeli forces in the southern Golan region of Syria; the shooter escaped and no Israeli casualties were reported (Source: @New_security8200/202140).
Israeli officials cited Iran's repeated calls for Israel's destruction, stating they are taking these statements seriously and informing the United States (Source: @amitsegal/62600).
Israel reported the killing of three senior Hamas commanders, including a naval police chief, in a strike on Gaza on Friday (Source: @kianmeli1/88219).
The Palestinian army carried out an operation detonating residential buildings near a mosque in Bani Suhail, east of Khan Younis, in the Gaza Strip (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373489).
Israeli security forces arrested an arms dealer and suspected terrorist in the Balata refugee camp, preventing an imminent attack on security forces and civilians (Source: @lelotsenzura/93292).
A 70‑year‑old Israeli citizen entered Area A illegally for shopping, was attacked, and his vehicle was stolen; he sustained minor injuries (Source: @yediotnews25/132022).

Hezbollah operatives engaged Israeli forces in Deir Siryan, resulting in the death of Cpt. David Hazutt and a subsequent IDF sweep that killed additional Hezbollah fighters (Source: @manniefabian/58695).
IDF airstrikes targeted Hezbollah sites in the Nabatieh area after operatives armed with RPGs were identified (Source: @manniefabian/58691).
The Israeli military demolished a large Hezbollah underground tunnel in Majdal Zoun, a strategic facility extending over 200 meters (Source: @manniefabian/58701).
Lebanese Parliament Speaker publicly rejected the current US‑Israel agreement, labeling it unacceptable and unsustainable (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373485).
A map update from a Lebanese source added a “yellow line” and mountain peaks, indicating possible changes in territorial delineations (Source: @redlinkleb/28848).
A militant fired at Israeli forces in the southern Golan Heights of Syria; the attacker fled and no Israeli casualties occurred (Source: @New_security8200/202140).
Unverified reports mention possible missile interceptions over Jordan and/or Syria, pending verification (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4483).
Iraqi security forces reportedly deployed extensively in Baghdad’s Green Zone, including tanks, amid heightened security concerns (Source: @asafroz/50819).
The United States and Iran announced a mutual halt to kinetic operations and scheduled a technical meeting in Qatar to discuss the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline/76734).
Iran’s commemoration of the Sardasht chemical attack included calls for Western accountability, reflecting regional diplomatic tensions (Source: @presstv/196058).

key_developments: IDF killed Cpt. David Hazutt and wounded another soldier during a clash with a Hezbollah gunman in Deir Siryan, southern Lebanon, then conducted strikes on Hezbollah positions (Source: @manniefabian/58695).
Israeli Air Force eliminated Hezbollah operatives armed with RPGs and destroyed a rocket launcher in the Nabatieh area, reducing immediate threat to troops (Source: @manniefabian/58691).
IDF demolished a 200‑meter Hezbollah underground tunnel in Majdal Zoun, a strategic facility extending over 25 meters deep (Source: @manniefabian/58701).
Hezbollah fired at Israeli forces on the Syrian Golan front; the shooter escaped without causing Israeli casualties (Source: @New_security8200/202140).
Israel struck and killed three senior Hamas commanders, including a naval police chief, in Gaza (Source: @kianmeli1/88219).
The Palestinian army detonated residential buildings near a mosque in Bani Suhail, east of Khan Younis, Gaza (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373489).
The United States and Iran reportedly agreed to cease kinetic operations and plan a Qatar meeting to address Strait of Hormuz issues (Source: @VahidOnline/76734).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to engage Israeli forces along the Lebanon border, employing small‑arms and RPGs, while also maintaining underground infrastructure that Israel is actively targeting. In Gaza, Israeli strikes against senior Hamas leadership indicate a focus on decapitating command structures, but Palestinian forces retain the capability to conduct urban detonations. The reported US‑Iran de‑escalation could reduce direct state‑level confrontation but does not eliminate proxy activities in the region.
sections.regional_response: Israel is intensifying its operational tempo in southern Lebanon and Gaza, combining kinetic strikes with intelligence‑driven demolitions of tunnels and weapon caches. Lebanon's political leadership remains divided over any US‑Israel agreement, reflecting internal resistance to external pressure. Iran's public commemoration of Sardasht and its diplomatic outreach to the US suggest a dual strategy of regional posturing and selective engagement.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah tunnel networks in southern Lebanon should prioritize satellite and SIGINT cues for new construction activity. The recent US‑Iran agreement may shift focus to maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz, requiring naval asset reallocation. Gaza's urban demolition tactics warrant close observation for civilian impact and potential escalation of retaliatory attacks.
